source: Rocky Linux 'About systemd' guide - Operational units

questions.q: Check whether the sshd service is set to start automatically at boot.
questions.why: systemctl is-enabled reports the boot-time enablement state of a unit.
systemctl mask links the unit to /dev/null so it cannot be started at all.

questions.q: How does masking a unit differ from merely disabling it?
options: A masked unit cannot be started at all (even manually); a disabled unit just won't start at boot
Masking starts the unit; disabling stops it
questions.why: disable removes the boot-time link but you can still start it by hand; mask makes it unstartable until unmasked.
systemctl unmask reverses a mask so the unit can run again.

questions.q: View the contents of the sshd.service unit file.
questions.why: systemctl cat prints the unit file (and any overrides).

options: Opens an override for the unit and saves it under /etc/systemd/system/
questions.why: systemctl edit creates/edits a drop-in override in /etc/systemd/system/ rather than touching the distributed unit file.

questions.q: Show the complete properties of the sshd service.
questions.why: systemctl show dumps all of a unit's properties as key=value pairs.
